focus_areas: Non-Lambertian depth estimation importance and EPI paradigm limitations
Motivation for physical modeling and full-view geometry
Explicit listing of the three core contributions

Introduction.target_emphasis: Establish the critical problem of Lambertian bottlenecks in EPI methods and introduce the proposed physics-driven, full-view framework as a systematic solution.

focus_areas: Evolution and limits of EPI-based light field depth estimation
The necessity of shifting from implicit EPI to explicit cost volumes
must_include: Critique of existing methods lacking physical interpretability for complex reflections (gap_bridge).
Transition statement explaining why frequency analysis and multi-view geometry are required to break the performance ceiling.

should_avoid: Making the section a standalone, disconnected literature review; it must be integrated with the problem context.
Listing papers without pointing out their specific limitations that the proposed method addresses.
Related Work.target_emphasis: Critically review existing depth estimation approaches to highlight the unmet need for physical modeling and full-perspective geometry in non-Lambertian scenes.

focus_areas: Dual-mask physical modeling and MRI-like frequency analysis (创新点1)
GCD descriptor and SAM-guided adaptive energy adjustment (创新点2)
MACVO-Net and multi-scale cost volume construction (创新点3)
must_include: Key formulas for 2D-DFT frequency spectrum conversion and component-aware confidence weighting.
Definition of the GCD (std/mean) and the specific scaling parameters (e.g., 15% suppression, 150% enhancement).
Detailed description (and preferably a horizontal flow architecture figure) of MACVO-Net utilizing all 81 views.
Clear justification of how each module mathematically and structurally addresses the limitations of EPI (contribution_claim).

Methodology.target_emphasis: Provide a rigorous, mathematically grounded explanation of the three-stage framework, seamlessly connecting physical reflection properties to network architecture design.

focus_areas: Quantitative and qualitative comparisons with SOTA methods
Ablation studies validating the individual and combined effects of the three innovations
Visualization of the physical parsing and energy maps
must_include: Comprehensive comparison tables showing MACVO-Net breaking the MAE lower limit (0.28) in Lambertian and Non-Lambertian scenes (evidence_support).
Ablation tables demonstrating the contribution of frequency analysis, SAM-guided energy adjustment, and the full-view cost volume.
Visual comparisons showing the elimination of 'smooth artifacts' and 'texture copying' effects.

should_avoid: Ignoring failure cases or algorithm limitations.

Experiments.target_emphasis: Empirically prove that the proposed physics-guided pipeline significantly outperforms existing methods by providing high-SNR inputs and resolving depth ambiguities.

focus_areas: Recap of the systematic breakthrough in EPI bottlenecks
Summary of theoretical and experimental successes
Critical analysis of limitations and future directions
must_include: Explicit statement on how the three proposed components successfully solve the targeted Lambertian bottleneck problem (contribution_claim).
Acknowledgment of algorithmic limitations, such as computational efficiency of 81-view processing or uncertainties in extreme scattering scenarios.

should_avoid: Failing to mention the algorithm's limitations or measurement uncertainties.
Conclusion.target_emphasis: Concisely synthesize the paper's success in bridging physics and deep learning for depth estimation, while transparently addressing its current boundaries.
